applied_optimizations.name: Triton kernel with 3D grid and tiled execution
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented MaxPool2D using Triton kernel with 3D grid (batch*channels, out_height/BLOCK_H, out_width/BLOCK_W). Each program handles a 16x256 tile of output elements, computing max over 4x4 kernel window. Key optimizations: (1) 3D grid to avoid 1D grid indexing overflow issues with large tensors, (2) BLOCK_H=16, BLOCK_W=256 rectangular tiles for optimal memory coalescing on AMD MI355X, (3) vectorized loads with proper masking for boundary handling.

attempted_but_failed.name: 1D grid with single thread per element
attempted_but_failed.reason: Grid indexing overflow with 534M elements caused incorrect output for batch_size >= 2

attempted_but_failed.name: 128x128 blocks
attempted_but_failed.reason: Slower (4.17ms) - too many registers per thread, reduced occupancy

attempted_but_failed.name: 4x1024 blocks
attempted_but_failed.reason: Slower (3.44ms) - too few threads in height dimension

top_bottlenecks: Memory bandwidth bound - each output element requires 16 loads for 4x4 kernel

notes: 3D grid is essential for large tensors on AMD - 1D grid with >33M elements produced incorrect results
Rectangular blocks (16x256) outperform square blocks (64x64) for this workload - 2.63ms vs 3.08ms
Optimal block size found through manual tuning: BLOCK_H=16, BLOCK_W=256
Achieved 2.46x speedup over PyTorch reference (2.63ms vs 6.46ms)
